sanskrit: तत्रैव सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः। पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः॥
transliteration: tatraiva sati kartārām ātmānaṃ kevalaṃ tu yaḥ | paśyaty akṛta-buddhitvān na sa paśyati durmatiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Being thus, one who sees the Self alone as the sole agent, owing to an unformed intellect—he, the mis-guided, does not see.
contextual_meaning: The ego that claims “I alone did it” blinds itself to the five-fold field of action; this grandiosity is a developmental arrest, not strength.
psychological_state: Narcissistic omnipotence—an unconscious defense against dependency and vulnerability.
trigger_scenario: Taking sole credit for a team success at work while dismissing mentors, colleagues, timing and luck.
anchor_text: True agency belongs to the field; humility dissolves the ego’s claim.
